title: get_adapter boxes zero-sized types per-request and silently falls back on typos
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Architectural Alignment / DIP

Location: src/providers/mod.rs line 120 (fn get_adapter).

Violation: get_adapter returns `Box<dyn ProviderAdapter>` constructed fresh on every call via `Box::new(AnthropicAdapter::new())`. This is called from proxy_chat_completions at line 688 on every single incoming request. Since each adapter is a zero-sized struct (e.g., `pub struct AnthropicAdapter;`), the Box allocation is wasteful BUT the bigger problem is that the trait objects are unnecessarily owned — callers cannot share a single static adapter. No caching layer exists.

Also:
  - Line 133-139: unknown provider silently falls back to OpenAIAdapter with a WARN log. This violates 'fail fast at system boundaries' — a typo in proxy.target (e.g., `anthrpic`) results in silently wrong behavior. The user pays for calls to the wrong endpoint.
  - The enum-like dispatch is hardcoded; adding a new provider requires editing this match. DIP violation.

Mandated Refactor:
  1. Make each adapter a `pub static ANTHROPIC: AnthropicAdapter = AnthropicAdapter;` (constructible at compile time — they're all ZSTs).
  2. Change get_adapter signature to `fn get_adapter(name: &str) -> Result<&'static dyn ProviderAdapter, ProviderError>`. No allocation, no Box.
  3. Return Err(ProviderError::Unsupported(format!("Unknown provider: {name}"))) for unrecognized names. No silent fallback. The proxy maps this to a 400 Bad Request with a helpful message listing supported providers.
  4. If a 'custom' OpenAI-compatible provider is needed (for LM Studio, LocalAI), make it an explicit config option (`providers.custom.api_format: openai`) rather than implicit fallback.
